data.title: Crown Castle: Strong Operating Beat and Fiber Divestiture Drive Focused U.S. Tower Strategy and Justify Buy Rating
data.description:
Crown Castle, the Real Estate sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Ari Klein from BMO Capital maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $100.00 price target.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ari Klein has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Crown Castle’s operating performance and strategic repositioning. He notes that quarterly AFFO and site rental revenue exceeded expectations, helped by stronger top-line trends, favorable expense timing, and lower interest costs, while organic growth and churn dynamics came in better than his projections.

Klein also emphasizes the pending fiber divestiture, which appears on schedule and supports Crown Castle’s transition toward a focused U.S. tower business with a cleaner balance sheet. In his view, prospective upside from the 2027 spectrum auction, selective new tower builds, and edge data center initiatives, combined with ongoing cost reductions, create an attractive risk/return profile that justifies maintaining a Buy recommendation.

Klein covers the Real Estate sector, focusing on stocks such as Equinix, Digital Realty, and Crown Castle. According to TipRanks, Klein has an average return of 6.3% and a 57.50% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on April 20, Bernstein also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $102.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
